title: neurocarpaea quadrangularis
intro: Neurocarpaea quadrangularis, also known as Devil's Claw or Grapple Plant, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Southern Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds, harpagoside and harpagide, which have been found to have analgesic and anti-rheumatic effects. The health benefits of Neurocarpaea quadrangularis include relief from arthritic pain, fibromyalgia, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Neurocarpaea quadrangularis is often prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, allowing for various forms of consumption to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
